title: Glass and ceramics makers, decorators and finishers
description: Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. Promote worksite or product safety by applying knowledge of industrial processes, mechanics, chemistry, psychology, and industrial health and safety laws. Includes industrial product safety engineers. Research causes of fires, determine fire protection methods, and design or recommend materials or equipment such as structural components or fire-detection equipment to assist organizations in safeguarding life and property against fire, explosion, and related hazards. (Based on 46 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Confer with clients regarding price, type of arrangement desired, and details of delivery.
Inspect lens blanks to detect flaws, verify smoothness, and ensure thickness of coating on lenses.
Repair broken parts using precision hand tools and soldering irons, and adjust lenses and frames to correct alignment.
Clean finished lenses and eyeglasses using cloths and solvents, and maintain portfolios of artistic work to demonstrate styles.
Select lens blanks, molds, tools, polishing wheels, or materials according to production specifications.
Set up and operate machines to polish, bevel, edge, or grind lenses and other precision optical elements.
Mount and secure lens blanks in holding tools or chucks of cutting or grinding machines, and trim material for arrangements using various tools.
Examine prescriptions, work orders, or broken glasses to determine specifications for lenses or other optical elements.
Assemble eyeglass frames and attach components using hand tools and perform general cleaning duties in the shop.
Perform office and retail service duties such as serving customers, answering phones, and maintaining financial records.
Inspect, measure, and weigh materials or products to verify conformance to specifications.
Observe machine operations, control boards, and gauges to detect malfunctions and ensure maintenance of production records.
Evaluate quality of finished workpieces by inspecting them and comparing their dimensions to templates.
Load specified amounts of materials into machines and adjust mechanisms to ensure proper operation.
Decorate or supervise the decoration of facilities for special occasions, and create and change in-store displays to enhance the shop's image.
Weigh or measure materials, ingredients, or products to ensure conformance to requirements.
Operate or tend machines to mix or blend materials such as food products, chemicals, or pigments.
Position equipment such as ladles, grinding wheels, pouring nozzles, or crucibles, or signal other workers for positioning.
Read temperature gauges, observe color changes, and adjust heating units as necessary to melt metal to specifications.
Dump or pour specified amounts of materials into machinery or equipment.
Tend accessory equipment, such as pumps or conveyors, to move materials or ingredients through production processes.
Weigh materials to be charged into furnaces and record operational or production data on specified forms.
Unloading mixtures into containers or onto conveyors for further processing and transferring materials between work areas.
Collect samples of materials or products for laboratory testing to ensure compliance with specifications.
Clean and maintain equipment, check for faults, and clear jams using hand tools.
Examine or test samples of processed substances or materials, ensuring conformance to established standards.
Mark identification numbers, trademarks, or grades on products.
Cut, shape, and trim materials using a variety of hand tools or machine tools.
Inspect materials, equipment, or work areas for defects or adherence to specifications and procedures.
Record production data, maintain logs, and monitor equipment operations for safety and efficiency.
Lay out and mark reference points and dimensions on materials, using measuring instruments and drawing or scribing tools.
Cut, shape, and form metal parts using lathes, power saws, snips, power brakes and shears, files, and mallets.
Inspect and test products to verify conformance to specifications using precision measuring instruments or circuit testers.
Set up and operate machines such as lathes, drill presses, punch presses, or bandsaws to fabricate prototypes or models.
Study blueprints, drawings, and sketches to determine material dimensions, required equipment, and operations sequences.
Calibrate liquid flow devices and meters, including fuel, chemical, and water meters; and verify the performance of biofuels processing data.
Operate chemical processing equipment for the production of biofuels and control biofuels production with valves and pumps.
Collect biofuels samples and perform routine laboratory tests or analyses to assess biofuels quality; monitor and record performance data.
Create or maintain formal engineering documents, such as schematics, bills of materials, or specifications for production.
Assemble pattern sections using hand tools, bolts, screws, rivets, glue, or welding equipment; and maintain finished components.
Determine commercial, industrial, scientific, or other uses for electro-optical applications or devices.
Develop laser-processed designs, such as laser-cut medical devices, and design laser machining equipment for purposes such as high-speed ablation.
Analyze, fabricate, or test fiber-optic links and provide engineering technical support to designers of prototype systems.
Debug robotics programs and conduct research related to robotic technology to create new robotic systems or capabilities.
Prepare and write reports detailing specific fire prevention and protection issues and communicate with bioregulatory authorities regarding compliance responsibilities.
Document robotic application development, maintenance, or changes and write proposals to secure external funding.
Inspect buildings or building designs to determine fire protection system requirements and evaluate potential health hazards from products.
Design fire detection equipment, alarm systems, and fire extinguishing devices while developing training materials on fire protection.
Conduct analyses addressing issues such as failure, reliability, or yield improvement in systems or products.
Investigate industrial accidents, injuries, or occupational diseases to determine causes and preventive measures.
Clean oxides and scales from parts or fittings, using steam sprays or chemical and water baths.
Adjust controls to maintain temperatures and heating times, using thermal instruments and charts, dials and gauges of furnaces.
Set and adjust speeds of reels and conveyors for prescribed time cycles to pass parts through continuous furnaces.
Mix and apply glazes to pottery pieces, using tools such as spray guns.
Operate gas or electric kilns to fire pottery pieces.
Engrave and print patterns, designs, or lettering onto surfaces of a wide variety of items using hand tools or power tools.
Inspect, weigh, and measure products to verify conformance to specifications using instruments such as micrometers and calipers.
Conduct or supervise tests on raw materials or finished products to ensure their quality.
Trim or remove excess material using scrapers, knives, or band saws.
Prepare work for sale or exhibition, and maintain relationships with networks that can facilitate sale or exhibition of work.
Operate drying chambers to dry or finish molded ceramic ware, and start machine units and conveyors while observing operational efficiency.
Adjust pressures, temperatures, and trimming tool settings as required, and adjust wheel speeds according to the feel of the clay.
Position balls of clay in centers of potters' wheels to start motors or pump treadles, and raise and shape clay into wares on revolving wheels.
Smooth surfaces of finished pieces using rubber scrapers and wet sponges, and examine finished ware for defects and measure dimensions with gauges.
Perform test-fires of pottery to achieve specific colors and textures, and maintain supplies of tools, equipment, and materials as needed.
Press and shape clay to desired forms by using hands and fingers, pulling wires to separate finished pieces, and moving pieces for drying.
Design molds and decorations for clay forms, and create functional or decorative objects by hand.
Operate and maintain finishing machines to grind, drill, sand, bevel, decorate or polish glass products, and shape or join sections of glass using appropriate tools.
Develop sketches of glass products into blueprints, determine types and quantities of glass required, and spray molds with oil solutions to prevent adhesion.
Set specifications for materials, dimensions, and finishes for products, and select materials based on characteristics like strength and texture.
Create prototypes or models of crafted objects, and develop concepts or ideas for craft objects and product packaging strategies.
Advertise products using various media, confer with customers to assess needs, and pack products for shipping.
Monitor machine cycles to ensure product conformity and troubleshoot equipment for minor repairs; clean work areas as needed.
Provide employee training in operation and safety procedures, and enforce safety regulations throughout the production area.
Evaluate and recommend measures to improve production methods, equipment performance, and product quality.
